core_model.structure_summary: A scalar Ito stochastic differential equation on the unit interval whose drift is a cubic (logistic-type) polynomial in the state and whose state-dependent diffusion coefficient vanishes at both endpoints, with all coefficients switched by an external finite-state continuous-time Markov jump process.
core_model.canonical_form: dx = x(1-x)\big[-B_{r} + (A_{r}+B_{r})x\big]\,dt + \sigma_{r}\, x(1-x)\, dW,\quad r=r(t)\text{ a finite-state Markov chain with generator }Q=(q_{ij})

variables.meaning: scalar fraction confined to the unit interval [0,1], a two-component simplex coordinate
scalar Brownian increment entering multiplicatively through the vanishing-at-boundary factor
drift coefficients (relative-fitness differences) and noise intensity, each indexed by the current mode
discrete mode of a finite-state continuous-time Markov chain that switches the coefficients, with transition-rate matrix Q

notes: Core is the 1-D switched replicator SDE dY(s,r); the vector A s(t) replicator ODE and the associated generator LV(x,i) are the deterministic/analytic tools around it. Extinction/boundary behavior on {0,1} is central to the results.
